title: chargeback — subprocessors
description: The Chargeback module itself engages ZERO third-party subprocessors — every state transition happens within the Stackra platform. Downstream payment module engages the actual payment providers (Stripe / Paddle / Adyen / Square / PayPal / CustomWebhook) that ORIGINATE the chargeback webhook + receive the evidence submission; downstream marketing module engages ad-network subprocessors (Meta / Google / TikTok / Microsoft / Snapchat) that receive negative-value + fraud signal events; downstream notifications module engages email/SMS subprocessors. All are declared in their respective modules' subprocessors.json.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Receiving chargeback webhooks (Stripe 'charge.dispute.created', Paddle 'transaction.chargeback', Adyen 'NOTIFICATION_OF_CHARGEBACK') + dispatching evidence submissions. RefundProviderExecutor's sibling — this module's EvidenceSubmitter delegates to payment::PaymentProviderManager which handles the provider-side HTTP call + webhook signature verification.
RDR path creates a Refund via refund::RefundOrchestrator. The refund module handles the provider-side refund dispatch through its own subprocessor chain (payment providers).

Forwarding Chargeback Filed (negative-value + fraud signal) events to ad networks (Meta CAPI, Google Ads Enhanced Conversions, TikTok Events API, Microsoft UET, Snapchat CAPI) for ROAS adjustment + fraud model retraining. Fraud model retraining is MORE aggressive for chargebacks than for refunds.

Delivering ChargebackFiledNotification + ChargebackEvidenceOverdueNotification + ChargebackWonNotification + ChargebackLostNotification + ChargebackRateThresholdExceededNotification + ChargebackEvidenceReminderNotification + ChargebackAcceptedRefundNotification via email / SMS / in-app / push.
CreditNoteIssuer creates the reversal credit note document on chargeback lost (tax reversal). Invoice module engages document rendering + storage subprocessors (S3 for PDF hosting).

Evidence auto-collection reads attendance records from attendance module (service_documentation evidence field). No third-party subprocessor per-se; the attendance module engages its own storage subprocessor for signature captures + attendance photos when applicable.

Evidence auto-collection reads support tickets + call recordings from support module (communication_log evidence field). Support module engages call recording storage subprocessor (S3 for audio recordings).
Retention pipeline (PurgeExpiredChargebackDataJob) migrates records to compliance_archive S3 buckets before hard-delete. Compliance module engages the archive-storage subprocessor.

notes: This file exists deliberately with an empty subprocessors array. The GDPR Art. 30 record of processing activities requires per-module subprocessor lists; an empty list is the correct declaration for a module that engages none directly.

The Wave 4 tenant-facing 'subprocessor disclosure' page reads THIS FILE'S downstream_subprocessors_referenced list to compose the full disclosure — every downstream that this module transitively engages is surfaced to the tenant admin.

Payment provider subprocessors (Stripe / Paddle / Adyen / Square / PayPal) are the HIGH-RISK category — they receive PCI-adjacent data (payment token, chargeback amount, evidence bundle). Chargeback does not directly engage them; the payment module handles the provider-side HTTP contracts + credential management + evidence submission dispatch.

Ad-network subprocessors (Meta / Google / TikTok / Microsoft / Snapchat) receive hashed PII + click IDs + chargeback amounts + reason categories on ChargebackLost. The fraud_signal boolean informs the ad network to weight the event more heavily for fraud model retraining. Tenants opt into which providers via marketing module's provider configuration.

Chargebacks are HIGH-STAKES for card network compliance — the entire subprocessor chain (payment providers + ad networks + evidence storage) must maintain integrity to preserve merchant-account standing.
